jokes.text: Oh, well, you know, my moves are kind of like top secret, Nick. I mean, I could show you, but then I'd have to kill you.
jokes.setup: Will talks about his basketball moves being 'top secret'
jokes.punchline: Classic spy movie trope applied to basketball tricks
jokes.explanation: Standard Will Smith charm - takes ordinary situation and adds his signature bravado with familiar reference

jokes.text: Now, this is called the Will Smith Hoop Dream, Dunk on Hakeem...Down the Lane, House of Pain pass.
jokes.setup: Will naming his basketball move
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously over-the-top rhyming name that references himself, Hakeem Olajuwon, and House of Pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will character moment - his ego, pop culture knowledge, and wordplay all in one absurdly named basketball move

jokes.text: Back in Philly, they used to call me Will 'The 747' Smith.
jokes.setup: Will claims he had a cool nickname involving an airplane
jokes.punchline: Because I'm about to jet
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed setup/punchline with good misdirection - audience expects basketball-related reason for nickname

jokes.text: Thad? I thought you were dating Kyle. I am. He's the flu.
jokes.setup: Confusion about Hilary dating multiple people
jokes.punchline: Kyle is her excuse ('the flu') to avoid Thad
jokes.explanation: Sharp writing that reveals Hilary's casual approach to dating through clever misdirection

jokes.text: Hi, Will. What you doing? Five to ten if you don't take your hands off me.
jokes.setup: Someone asks Will what he's doing
jokes.punchline: Prison sentence reference instead of normal activity
jokes.explanation: Quick wit from Will turning innocent question into threat about unwanted touching

jokes.text: Hill, what you think? You did a pretty good show today, huh? Three words: Stink. Stank. Stunk.
jokes.setup: Will asks for Hilary's opinion on the show
jokes.punchline: Hilary's brutal three-word review using rhyming past tenses
jokes.explanation: Memorable wordplay that became quotable - creative way to say something was terrible

jokes.text: Will and assistant engage in circular logic about responsibility and getting better guests
jokes.setup: Will complains about not having responsibility
jokes.punchline: Extended circular conversation where each person won't commit first
jokes.explanation: Functional workplace comedy but too drawn out for the payoff

jokes.text: Don't make me turn the hose on you, all right?
jokes.setup: Someone being overly affectionate/flirty with Will
jokes.punchline: Will threatens to spray them like a misbehaving pet
jokes.explanation: Classic Will deflection with pet training reference

jokes.text: Would you like to buy a vowel?
jokes.setup: Will sees Uncle Phil looking stressed/confused
jokes.punchline: Wheel of Fortune reference suggesting Phil looks puzzled
jokes.explanation: Simple game show reference, functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Oh, really? Hey, did you check under your robe?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil lost a juror
jokes.punchline: Will suggests looking under his judge's robe like losing keys
jokes.explanation: Clever literalization of 'losing' a juror, treating it like misplacing an object

jokes.text: You sure he wasn't just downloading his floppy?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentions juror was caught with laptop in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Computer/bathroom double entendre with 1990s tech reference
jokes.explanation: Solid tech-era innuendo that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: Carlton's dating service video being overly forward and confident
jokes.setup: Carlton recording dating video
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at being a smooth ladies' man
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton cringe - his earnest attempt at being cool is inherently funny

jokes.text: Hm. A tad forward.
jokes.setup: Carlton's over-the-top dating video
jokes.punchline: His understated self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Great character beat - Carlton's obliviousness to how inappropriate he sounded

jokes.text: That felt close.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying another approach in his dating video
jokes.punchline: His delusional self-assessment that he almost got it right
jokes.explanation: Carlton's continued delusion about his dating video performance

jokes.text: What is your problem, Pepe Le Puny?
jokes.setup: Carlton being pretentious in his dating video
jokes.punchline: Reference to Pepe Le Pew but calling him 'puny'
jokes.explanation: Decent cartoon reference with size joke about Carlton

jokes.text: Of course you do. That's my Dad, you half-life.
jokes.setup: Will says he knows someone on TV
jokes.punchline: Carlton calls Will 'half-life' instead of half-wit
jokes.explanation: Smart Carlton insult using science terminology instead of common insult

jokes.text: The dude that cut that jack-o'-lantern in your head last Halloween?
jokes.setup: Jazz mentions his barber
jokes.punchline: Will describes Jazz's haircut as looking like a carved pumpkin
jokes.explanation: Vivid, creative insult that creates funny mental image

jokes.text: It'd have been great if that candle hadn't burned the roof of my mouth.
jokes.setup: Will's jack-o'-lantern haircut joke
jokes.punchline: Jazz acts like he literally had a candle in his pumpkin head
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that takes the visual metaphor literally in an unexpected way

jokes.text: Why not? She got a nice big head.
jokes.setup: Will asks if barber would work on Hilary
jokes.punchline: Jazz comments on Hilary's head size (both literal and ego)
jokes.explanation: Simple insult with double meaning - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Now, you know I'm gonna have to charge you extra for working around these ears.
jokes.setup: Will enters barber shop
jokes.punchline: Barber immediately insults Will's ear size
jokes.explanation: Standard physical feature insult from new character

jokes.text: The idiot that put the candle in his mouth?
jokes.setup: Will mentions Jazz
jokes.punchline: Barber remembers Jazz's literal interpretation of jack-o'-lantern haircut
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the earlier absurd moment

jokes.text: First, you got Geraldo coming in here...you know his head is as empty as Al Capone's vault
jokes.setup: George complaining about talk show hosts
jokes.punchline: References Geraldo's infamous Al Capone vault special that found nothing
jokes.explanation: Sharp reference to actual TV disaster that perfectly fits the insult

jokes.text: I told Montel, 'Yeah, I'll do your show when you grow some hair.'
jokes.setup: George continuing to reject talk show hosts
jokes.punchline: Insult about Montel Williams being bald
jokes.explanation: Basic physical feature insult, less clever than the Geraldo one

jokes.text: We got cash. You got George.
jokes.setup: George refuses to do tabloid TV, mentions dignity and self-respect
jokes.punchline: Instantly changes tune when money is mentioned
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy - shows how quickly principles disappear for money

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, just yuk it up. He who laughs last... See, it's started already.
jokes.setup: Carlton being mocked about his dating service
jokes.punchline: Gets interrupted mid-threat, showing he can't even finish a comeback
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's inability to complete a confident threat

jokes.text: You're in my thoughts. Love, Thad.
jokes.setup: Carlton expects romantic interest from dating service
jokes.punchline: Gets flowers from male admirer instead
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection about Carlton's dating service results

jokes.text: Please, for carnations and a little baby's breath? Nothing.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks what to do about male admirer's flowers
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey dismisses cheap flowers as not worth responding to
jokes.explanation: Good Geoffrey snark about flower quality standards

jokes.text: So there's no sense of me playing, is there?
jokes.setup: Will says Carlton will never guess who's on the show
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately gives up on guessing game
jokes.explanation: Carlton's logical but deflating response to rhetorical setup

jokes.text: Hey, Nicky, you will never guess who we got... How many times do I have to tell you kids? I just can't talk about it.
jokes.setup: Will starts to tell Nicky about guest
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil cuts him off thinking it's about the trial
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Uncle Phil's paranoia about the trial

jokes.text: Then I can't discuss your radio. Back of the line.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil can't discuss his case
jokes.punchline: Store employee uses same logic to refuse service
jokes.explanation: Good logical reversal using Phil's own words against him. Smart structure

jokes.text: Why should I be nervous? I'm all liquored up.
jokes.setup: Will asks if George is nervous about TV appearance
jokes.punchline: George admits to drinking before the show
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that sets up George as unreliable TV guest

jokes.text: That lard-butt of a judge. That man is crazy as they come.
jokes.setup: George on Hilary's show talking about the judge
jokes.punchline: Insults Uncle Phil not knowing it's Hilary's father
jokes.explanation: Strong dramatic irony as audience knows what George doesn't

jokes.text: Couldn't judge a beauty pageant. About as useless as a no-mouthed dog at a Frisbee contest.
jokes.setup: George continuing to insult Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Creative analogy about a mouthless dog playing Frisbee
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and vivid insult that became memorable

jokes.text: If I ever see him, I'll lower him like a cheap carpet.
jokes.setup: George threatening the judge
jokes.punchline: Says 'lower him like a cheap carpet' instead of 'lay him out'
jokes.explanation: Creative threat mixing up common phrases in amusing way

jokes.text: Ain't that right, Will Smith?
jokes.setup: George has been insulting Uncle Phil on live TV
jokes.punchline: Directly involves Will by name, making him complicit
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that traps Will and creates maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: And if not, I'll just blame the whole thing on you. Oops. Did I say that out loud?
jokes.setup: Hilary and Will discussing Uncle Phil's reaction
jokes.punchline: Hilary accidentally reveals her plan to blame Will
jokes.explanation: Good Hilary selfishness reveal with direct audience acknowledgment

jokes.text: Oh, not bad for a lard butt, huh?
jokes.setup: Hilary compliments Uncle Phil's appearance
jokes.punchline: Phil sarcastically uses George's insult from the show
jokes.explanation: Effective callback showing Phil's hurt through sarcasm

jokes.text: And also that it's not called The Will Show.
jokes.setup: Will trying to avoid blame
jokes.punchline: Points out it's not his show to deflect responsibility
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the show's name while deflecting blame

jokes.text: And also that it's not called The Will Show.
jokes.setup: Will trying to deflect blame
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the show isn't named after him
jokes.explanation: Weak deflection attempt that barely works - shows Will grasping for excuses

jokes.text: Daddy, punish him. He needs tough love.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil angry at both Will and Hilary
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately throwing Will under the bus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary move - immediately abandoning solidarity to save herself

jokes.text: Technically, Uncle Phil, I'm not really your flesh and blood. See, my Mom and Aunt Viv, they...
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil talks about family disappointing him
jokes.punchline: Will tries to technically exclude himself from family
jokes.explanation: Classic Will deflection attempt using literal interpretation

jokes.text: I don't have any common sense.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks if they have common sense
jokes.punchline: Hilary matter-of-factly admits she doesn't
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - completely honest about her limitations

jokes.text: Yesterday, a dismissed juror in the Show-Biz Madam Trial...came on the show and said some really icky things about him.
jokes.setup: Hilary describing the serious insults to her father
jokes.punchline: Calls devastating public humiliation 'icky things'
jokes.explanation: Hilary's childlike vocabulary for serious situation

jokes.text: You should be ashamed of yourself. Ow!
jokes.setup: Hilary's emotional TV apology
jokes.punchline: Audience member calls her out and apparently gets hit
jokes.explanation: Unexpected audience hostility adds to the cringe factor

jokes.text: Oh, no, not another rejection. Bad news? I sent this to Last Chance Video Dating. You know, 'We guarantee you a date,' except if your name is Carlton Banks.
jokes.setup: Carlton getting package from dating service
jokes.punchline: Even 'Last Chance' service rejected him specifically
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Carlton's dating failures - even desperate services won't take him

jokes.text: I wouldn't expect someone like you to have trouble meeting people. The only people I meet are dogs and shut-ins.
jokes.setup: Woman surprised Carlton has dating trouble
jokes.punchline: Carlton's pathetic description of his social circle
jokes.explanation: Carlton's honest assessment of his limited social interactions

jokes.text: You know, 'We guarantee you a date,' except if your name is Carlton Banks.
jokes.setup: Carlton got another rejection from dating service
jokes.punchline: Realizes the guarantee has an exception specifically for him
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware Carlton moment about his dating struggles. Well-structured punchline

jokes.text: Sweet mother of God, that's a firm handshake.
jokes.setup: Carlton shaking hands with delivery woman
jokes.punchline: His dramatic reaction to her strong handshake
jokes.explanation: Carlton's overwrought reaction to simple physical interaction

jokes.text: Tell me, what do you have planned for tomorrow? Perhaps snapshots of me in the bathtub, huh? Or a video of me chasing stray dogs in my car.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil angry about continued public humiliation
jokes.punchline: Imagines increasingly absurd future embarrassments
jokes.explanation: Phil's paranoid imagination creates funny absurd scenarios

jokes.text: Daddy, you don't do that, do you?
jokes.setup: Phil mentions chasing stray dogs sarcastically
jokes.punchline: Hilary takes it seriously and worries it's real
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary reaction showing her literal-mindedness and concern for appearances

jokes.text: Daddy, you don't do that, do you?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil's absurd example of chasing dogs in his car
jokes.punchline: Ashley's innocent question taking it literally
jokes.explanation: Ashley's innocent literalism adds to the absurdity

jokes.text: Boy, here's a family that really puts the 'fun' back in dysfunctional, isn't it?
jokes.setup: TV host commenting on Banks family drama
jokes.punchline: Clever wordplay about dysfunction/fun
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that comments on the family's public embarrassment

jokes.text: Will performs 'And I Am Telling You I'm Not Going' from Dreamgirls
jokes.setup: Will desperately trying to apologize to Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Performs emotional Broadway song to show he won't leave the family
jokes.explanation: Bold choice to have Will Smith perform Dreamgirls song - unexpected and emotionally effective
